channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Argentina's Milei skirts Congress to seat a divisive judge in the Supreme Court
item.description: Argentina’s President Javier Milei has temporarily appointed two Supreme Court judges by decree, bypassing Congress during its summer recess to push through a controversial candidate in a move criticized as an overreach of executive power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Botswana, the country with the biggest diamonds, gets an improved 10-year deal with miner De Beers
item.description: GABORONE, Botswana (AP) — The Botswana government signed a new diamond sales agreement crucial to its economy with the De Beers mining company on Tuesday after seven years of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: SELLO MOTSETA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New search to begin for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370: Reports
item.description: The latest search comes after the Malaysian government in December agreed to accept a second "no find, no fee" proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Syria's national dialogue conference held as the battered country seeks to rebuild
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria’s new rulers held a long-awaited national dialogue conference Tuesday, which they touted as as a “rare historical opportunity” to rebuild the country after the fall of former President Bashar Assad and nearly 14 years of civil war.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Power outage in Chile leaves millions without electricity as officials scramble for answers
item.description: A widespread power outage in the South American nation of Chile left millions of people without electricity Tuesday, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage briefs UN Security Council on the 'pure hell' that was captivity in Gaza
item.description: Former Hamas hostage Noa Argamani spoke to the U.N. Security Council on Tuesday about her time as a captive in Gaza. She also made a desperate plea for the release of the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: Three senior Ukrainian officials say that Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals.
item.dc:creator: Susie Blann And Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical but stable condition Tuesday as he worked from the hospital while battling double pneumonia, and the Vatican announced some major governing decisions that suggest he is getting essential work done and looking ahead.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis in critical but stable condition, has CT scan, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis remains in critical condition but is stable and returned to work Tuesday evening, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A federal judge in Seattle blocks Trump’s effort to halt the refugee admissions system
item.description: A federal judge in Seattle has blocked President Donald Trump’s effort to halt the nation’s refugee admissions system.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kremlin appears to contradict Trump on Ukraine peacekeepers
item.description: The Kremlin appeared to contradict President Trump’s claim that Russia would accept peacekeeping troops in Ukraine. “There is a position on this matter that was expressed by Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov,” Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said, according to Politico.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Supporters of separatist Bosnian Serb president Dodik rally as he awaits verdict fueling tensions
item.description: BANJA LUKA, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — Supporters of separatist Bosnian Serb President Milorad Dodik rallied on Tuesday as tensions soared on the eve of a scheduled court verdict that could order the pro-Russia Serb leader banned from politics or even sentenced to prison.
item.dc:creator: RADUL RADOVANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin ready to work with US companies on mining rare-earth deposits in Russian-occupied Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said his nation was ready to work with U.S. companies to secure rare raw minerals within the nation’s borders in hopes of ending the conflict in Ukraine after three years of war.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Medics say 6 babies have died from the cold in Gaza as displaced people shelter in tents and rubble
item.description: Palestinian medics say at least six infants have died from hypothermia in the last two weeks in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trial begins in Germany for 4 alleged Hamas members accused of seeking weapons caches across Europe
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A trial began Tuesday in Germany for four alleged Hamas members suspected of organizing weapons caches across Europe. The country's top prosecutor accuses the men of membership in a foreign terrorist organization. It’s a pilot case for prosecutors, German news agency dpa reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan coast guard detains Chinese-crewed vessel suspected of cutting undersea cable
item.description: Taiwan detained eight Chinese nationals crewing a ship suspected of severing an undersea cable connecting the island of Penghu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US votes against condemning Russia for Ukraine war as Trump admin chases peace deal
item.description: The U.S. voted against a U.N. resolution laying blame on Russia for its invasion of Ukraine and demanding Moscow remove its troops from Ukrainian territory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: US refuses to blame Russia for Ukraine war, splitting with European allies in UN votes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions Monday seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korean hackers behind $1.5 billion crypto hack: Security firm
item.description: North Korean hackers were behind a sophisticated hack that stole about $1.5 billion worth of digital currency from Bybit, a major cryptocurrency exchange, according to security firm TRM.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American tourist arrested in Paris after allegedly throwing newborn baby out of hotel window: reports
item.description: Paris authorities arrested an American woman on Monday after she allegedly killed her newborn by hurling the child out of a second-floor window, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Macron project unity despite clear Ukraine-Russia divide
item.description: President Trump and French President Emmanuel Macron sought to project unity during a White House meeting Monday, even as cracks opened further in the alliance between the U.S. and Europe over the path forward in Ukraine. The two met at the Oval Office as the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Australian treasurer seeking tariff exemption in talks with Bessent
item.description: Australia’s treasurer, Jim Chalmers, will continue to push for steel and aluminum tariff exemptions in his discussions with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent this week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: These 17 countries voted with US against Ukraine UN resolution
item.description: Seventeen countries in the United Nations General Assembly joined the U.S. on Monday in voting against a nonbinding resolution condemning Russia as the aggressor in the ongoing war in Ukraine — more than double the vote against a similar resolution two years ago.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Germany will likely continue its leadership on climate action. But expect a change in tone
item.description: As Germany prepares for a change of government, climate experts are hopeful that Sunday's election results mean that the country will continue to be a leader in ambitious efforts to slash planet-warming emissions and will keep growing its renewable energy industries.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian consulate in France targeted with explosive projectiles on 3rd anniversary of Ukraine invasion
item.description: Two explosive projectiles detonated outside the Russian consulate in Marseille, France, on the third anniversary of Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The biggest takeaways from Germany's election, which will bring change to the EU's leading power
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany faces its second change of leader in fewer than four years after the head of the center-right opposition, Friedrich Merz, won Sunday's election, which saw a surge for a far-right party and a stinging defeat for outgoing Chancellor Olaf Scholz.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu decries release of wrong body as a ceasefire violation. Hamas pledges to investigate
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed revenge Friday for what he described as a “cruel and malicious violation” of the ceasefire agreement after authorities determined that a body released by Hamas was not an Israeli mother of two small boys, as the militant group had prom
item.dc:creator: AREEJ HAZBOUN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 20 Mexican cities, 5 in US among 50 most dangerous in the world
item.description: At the top of the list is Haiti's Port-au-Prince with 139 homicides per 100,000 residents, followed by five cities in Mexico: Colima, Manzanillo, Acapulco, Tijuana and Ciudad Obregón.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation
